items.title: Delimitation & Electoral Roll Update
items.simpleExplanation: Boundaries are checked and the voter list is updated to add new voters and remove dead/shifted voters.
items.beginnerExplanation: Cleaning up the list of who can vote and where.
items.commonConfusion: People think they only need to register once forever. Actually, if you move to a new city, you must update your address.
items.officialReminder: Check the final electoral roll published by your State Election Commission.

items.title: Nomination & Scrutiny
items.simpleExplanation: Candidates file papers to run. The Election Commission checks if their papers are legally valid.
items.beginnerExplanation: Checking if the people who want to be leaders follow all the rules to apply.
items.commonConfusion: People think anyone can just stand for election on voting day. You have to apply weeks in advance and pay a deposit.
items.officialReminder: Affidavits showing candidate criminal records and assets are made public.

items.title: Public Campaigning
items.simpleExplanation: Parties hold rallies, distribute manifestos, and ask for votes.
items.beginnerExplanation: Politicians explain their plans to the public.
items.commonConfusion: People think campaigning can happen until the moment they vote. It actually stops 48 hours before voting begins.
items.officialReminder: The Model Code of Conduct is strictly enforced. Report violations via cVIGIL.

items.title: Voting Day
items.simpleExplanation: Citizens go to designated booths to securely cast their vote using EVMs.
items.beginnerExplanation: The day you actually go press the button for your chosen leader.
items.commonConfusion: Voters sometimes forget their Voter ID and think they can't vote. Other official IDs like Aadhaar or PAN are also accepted.
items.officialReminder: Photography inside the polling booth is a punishable offense.

items.title: EVM Unsealing & Counting
items.simpleExplanation: EVMs are brought from strong rooms and votes are tallied securely.
items.beginnerExplanation: Adding up all the button presses to see who got the most.
items.commonConfusion: People sometimes trust unverified WhatsApp forwards for results. Only the ECI releases official tallies.
items.officialReminder: Real-time trends are published officially at results.eci.gov.in.

items.title: Declaration & Government Formation
items.simpleExplanation: The winner is given a certificate, and the party with the most seats forms the government.
items.beginnerExplanation: The official handover of power to the winners.
items.commonConfusion: If no one party gets a majority, they can form teams (coalitions) to reach the required number.
items.officialReminder: The Election Commission's role ends once results are certified; the President/Governor handles government formation.
